title: Bytes, Encodings, and Cryptographic Primitive Selection · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds byte representation and primitive decision table, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of byte representation and primitive decision table while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace strings, UTF-8, bytes, hexadecimal, base64, binary fields, lengths, and canonical encodings without changing their security meaning.
Distinguish encoding, checksums, hashing, MACs, signatures, encryption, key agreement, KEMs, and password hashing by purpose and reversibility.
Choose a vetted high-level construction that supplies the required combined properties instead of composing low-level primitives casually.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Bytes Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 9384 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate encoding, hashing, authentication, confidentiality, reversibility, and canonical domain-bound bytes; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace strings, UTF-8, bytes, hexadecimal, base64, binary fields, lengths, and canonical encodings without changing their security meaning.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Bytes Text Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace strings, utf-8, bytes, hexadecimal, base64, binary fields, lengths, and canonical encodings without changing their security meaning. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Bytes Text Boundary, keep Crypto Go Bytes Text Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
